projectDescription: A Raspberry Pi-based vertical plotter that reads g-code files and drives stepper motors and a servo pen-lift via wiringPi to draw on a vertical canvas.
keyFeatures: Reads standard g-code files (compatible with Inkscape gcodetools)
Drives dual stepper motors via EasyDriver boards using wiringPi GPIO
Supports linear moves (G0/G1), clockwise arcs (G2), and counter-clockwise arcs (G3)
Feed rate control for controlled drawing speed
Configurable canvas geometry (origin offsets, motor base length, steps/mm)
publicApiSurface: --x0=<mm>          horizontal offset from left motor to canvas origin
--y0=<mm>          vertical offset from left motor to canvas origin
--steps=<n>        steps per mm (1600 / pulley circumference for microsteps)
G0 X Y             rapid move (pen up)
G1 X Y F           linear move with feed rate
G2 X Y R / I J F   clockwise arc
G3 X Y R / I J F   counter-clockwise arc
M30                end program and return to origin
